name: United States v. Lovasco
question: By indicting Lovasco for crimes he allegedly committed 18 months prior, did the government violate either: (1) the defendant’s Sixth Amendment right to a speedy trial or (2) his due process rights guaranteed by the Fifth Amendment?
description: A case in which the Court held that the Sixth Amendment right to a speedy trial applies only after a person has been accused of a crime and that the pre-indictment delay did not deprive Lovasco of due process, though the lapse of time somewhat prejudiced his defense.
